notes: Released August 24, 1996; added Windows 95 UI; IIS 2.0; PPTP; DCOM. Succeeded by Windows 2000 Server.
Main NT Server competitor; 63% NOS market share at peak in early 1990s; declining by 1996.
Bundled with NT Server 4.0; Microsoft claims 40% faster than IIS 1.0; supports Web, gopher, FTP, ODBC.
Enables virtual private networks over public Internet; new in NT Server 4.0.
Extends COM for cross-network component communication; uses same tools as COM.
Adopted as standard by major networking vendors; promised as NT Server 5.0 core directory technology.
NT Server competitor; IBM continued development after Microsoft exit; lost NOS market battle.
NT 4.0 tuned for 4-way SMP; NT scales to 32 processors.
